CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT JORGE CABALLERO AKA JORGE DE JESUS CABALLERO ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 8.0 HOURS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JORGE CABALLERO'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT FOR LACK OF NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT LACKED ACTUAL NOTICE WHICH WAS NOT CAUSED BY AVOIDANCE OR INEXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE CONTRERAS DECLARATION SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVE OTHERWISE. DEFENDANT TIMELY FILED THE INSTANT MOTION UPON DISCOVERING THE LITIGATION. DEFENDANT HAS MET THE REQUIREMENTS TO SET ASIDE A DEFAULT UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473.5. FURTHER, IT APPEARS THAT A REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT WAS MADE PREMATURELY ON MAY 2, 2011. EVEN IF THE COMPLAINT HAD BEEN SERVED ON APRIL 8, 2011 BY SUBSTITUTED SERVICE AND MAIL, IT WAS NOT COMPLETED UNTIL APRIL 18, 2011. (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 415.20.) THUS, THE RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT WAS NOT DUE UNTIL THIRTY DAYS HAD ELAPSED ON MAY 20, 2011. (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 412.20(A)(3).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

Ntc Of Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgment For Lack Iof Ntc
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEKSANDR A. VOLKOV, TEL. NO. 987-7000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JORGE CABALLERO'S Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgment For Lack of Notice is Granted. Defendant lacked actual notice which was not caused by avoidance or inexcusable neglect. The Contreras declaration submitted by Defendant does not prove otherwise. Defendant timely filed the instant motion upon discovering the litigation. Defendant has met the requirements to set aside a default under Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5. Further, it appears that a request for entry of default was made prematurely on May 2, 2011. Even if the complaint had been served on April 8, 2011 by substituted service and mail, it was not completed until April 18, 2011. (Code of Civil Procedure section 415.20.) Thus, the response to the complaint was not due until thirty days had elapsed on May 20, 2011. (Code of Civil Procedure section 412.20(a)(3).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)
